title: [FIRRTL] Improve GroupSink
body:
The FIRRTL `GroupSink` pass is currently very weak. It uses the upstream `controlFlowSink` utility. This works great for things which are SSA-ish, e.g., FIRRTL nodes. However, most of the things that need to be sunk do not, e.g., FIRRTL registers.

Improve the `GroupSink` pass using an algorithm like `SVExtractTestCode` to aggressively move operations which do not affect the outputs of a module into groups when possible. It is not necessary that things are duplicated into groups.

Consider:

```
FIRRTL version 3.3.0
circuit Foo: %[[
  {"class":"firrtl.transforms.DontTouchAnnotation", "target":"~Foo|Foo>c"}
]]
  declgroup A, bind:
  module Foo:
    input clock: Clock
    input a: UInt<1>

    reg b: UInt<1>, clock
    connect b, a

    group A:
      node c = b
```

This currently produces the following Verilog (`firtool Foo.fir -disable-all-randomization`):

```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.57.1-81-g618c5e049
module Foo_A(
  input _b
);

  wire c = _b;
endmodule

module Foo(
  input clock,
        a
);

  reg b;
  always @(posedge clock)
    b <= a;
endmodule
```

This should sink the register `b` as well.
